name: No Time For Love
description:
She's working in a hell called "9 to 5 job" for years. She's living for the sake of living

I'll give you guys a special

Once I hit 5k followers, I will make the character definitions public of ONE BOT of mine

And it will be on a majority vote

I'll make a short tutorial on how I make my bots

(Impossible goal: I am perpetually on 0 Followers😈😈)

Okay, but for real though, I will actually public one of my bots' definitions and do a short tutorial that probably won't help anyone, once I hit that goal (I have a way of keeping track of it)

I want your opinions on the placeholder image. Imo, it's really fucking shit, they could've done a generic anime woman for bots with female tags(add two, when multiple + female) and generic anime man for male tags(add two, when multiple + male), if both then use both pictures

to be honest, that wolf image... just feels like a mockery to the users and creators, especially for bots where creators have left (meaning impossible to be changed, unless the creator comes back)

I fully expect arguments to start because of the text below. Although I wouldn't want it, I did want to vent a bit and sometimes making counterarguments just helps me vent (for some reason)

I already hate people defending censorship so I'll list down their arguments and counter them

> It's to prevent CSAM

  • Nudity does not equal CSAM. It still exists, nothing has changed (since those can get censored by the users, like what other creators are doing)

> To put it on the app store

  • That would mean censoring/filtering the descriptions and roleplay, which goes against their promise of keeping NSFW content

> I like the change because I don't have to see explicit images/porn images anymore

  • there's already a BLUR OPTION that is ON by default once you make an account on this site. If you don't want to see it, why did you turn it off? since most blurred images would be porn in the first place

> The site could get taken down for CSAM (adding more to the earlier CSAM argument)

  • then why keep the descriptions uncensored? If the thumbnails had a chance of taking this site down, then the descriptions absolutely would also get this taken down

> It's only a small change, why fret over it

  • Because that change is COMPLETELY pointless, and the change is actually worse than we imagined because of bots from creators that left

  • if this change was truly insignificant, why was it made at all? If it doesn’t affect users much, then it shouldn’t affect the platform much either... so why bother?

> People are overreacting

  • any platforms that allowed NSFW from the start had content slowly restricted or outright banned over time. Initially a "small" restriction like removing NSFW thumbnails (content that was slowly restricted or banned, non-chatbot examples: Tumblr, Patreon’s shifting policies, etc., And chatbot examples: Yodayo and Character AI).

> The site is free (I hate this one the most since it tries to silence criticism in one of the shittiest way possible)

  • Just because something is free doesn’t mean users have no right to criticize it. Even if JanitorAI is free, users contribute by creating bots, engaging with the site, and keeping the platform alive. If a platform thrives because of its user base, then that user base has every right to demand transparency and fair policies. There are so many people saying "Freedom of Speech" as a defense for 'criticism' on NTR bots, but oh when it's suddenly criticism of the TOS, then you SHOULDN'T complain about it !!!

  • Take Youtube for example. Youtube is free, but people complained, and no one stopped their complaints. They still face backlash
creator: NotHorny
